3. (In Android Recovery) Use volume down to highlight "Wipe Data/Factory Reset"

7. Use volume down to highlight "Reboot System Now"

Row: 936 _id=2845, thread_id=489, address=+18007267864, person=NULL, date=1770946039807, date_sent=1770946004000, protocol=0, read=1, status=-1, type=1, reply_path_present=0, subject=proto:CjoKImNvbS5nb29nbGUuYW5kcm9pZC5hcHBzLm1lc3NhZ2luZy4SFCIAKhAT5wGREvlMYYkUJfEhCyPt, body=To restore a backup on a Samsung Galaxy phone, you have several options depending on where your data was saved. The main methods are Samsung Cloud, Smart Switch, and Google Account.

Restore via Samsung Cloud
Open Settings → Accounts and backup → Samsung Cloud.

Tap Restore data.

Select the device backup you want to restore.

Choose the data types (apps, contacts, messages, settings, etc.).

Tap Restore and wait for the process to complete.

Restore via Smart Switch
Install Smart Switch on both your old and new Samsung devices (or on a PC/Mac).

Connect devices via cable or Wi-Fi.

On the new phone, open Smart Switch → Receive data → choose source (Galaxy/Android/PC).

Select the backup and tap Restore.

Restore via Google Account

Ensure backups are available in your Google account.
